title: chore(deps): bump the npm_and_yarn group across 1 directory with 2 updates
body:
Bumps the npm_and_yarn group with 2 updates in the / directory: [@astrojs/vercel](https://github.com/withastro/astro/tree/HEAD/packages/integrations/vercel) and [astro](https://github.com/withastro/astro/tree/HEAD/packages/astro). Updates `@astrojs/vercel` from 9.0.5 to 10.0.4
Release notes

Sourced from @​astrojs/vercel's releases.

@​astrojs/vercel@​10.0.4

Patch Changes

  • #16170 d0fe1ec Thanks @​bittoby! - Fixes edge middleware next() dropping the HTTP method and body when forwarding requests to the serverless function, which caused non-GET API routes (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) to return 404

@​astrojs/vercel@​10.0.3

Patch Changes

  • #16079 aa26636 Thanks @​empoulter-uclan! - Fix vercel ISR path rewrite

@​astrojs/vercel@​10.0.2

Patch Changes

  • #15959 335a204 Thanks @​matthewp! - Fix Vercel serverless path override handling so override values are only applied when the trusted middleware secret is present.

@​astrojs/vercel@​10.0.1

Patch Changes

  • #15934 6f8f0bc Thanks @​ematipico! - Updates the Astro peerDependencies#astro to be 6.0.0.

@​astrojs/vercel@​10.0.0

Major Changes

  • #15413 736216b Thanks @​florian-lefebvre! - Removes the deprecated @astrojs/vercel/serverless and @astrojs/vercel/static exports. Use the @astrojs/vercel export instead

Minor Changes

  • #15258 d339a18 Thanks @​ematipico! - Stabilizes the adapter feature experimentalStatiHeaders. If you were using this feature in any of the supported adapters, you'll need to change the name of the flag:

    export default defineConfig({
  adapter: netlify({
-    experimentalStaticHeaders: true
+    staticHeaders: true
  })
})

  • #15413 736216b Thanks @​florian-lefebvre! - Updates the implementation to use the new Adapter API

  • #15495 5b99e90 Thanks @​leekeh! - Adds new middlewareMode adapter feature and deprecates edgeMiddleware option

    The edgeMiddleware option is now deprecated and will be removed in a future release, so users should transition to using the new middlewareMode feature as soon as possible.

    export default defineConfig({
  adapter: vercel({
-    edgeMiddleware: true
+    middlewareMode: 'edge'
  })

Release notes

Sourced from astro's releases.

astro@6.1.8

Patch Changes

  • #16367 a6866a7 Thanks @​ematipico! - Fixes an issue where build output files could contain special characters (!, ~, {, }) in their names, causing deploy failures on platforms like Netlify.

  • #16381 217c5b3 Thanks @​ematipico! - Slightly improved the performance of the dev server by caching the internal crawling of the dependencies of a project.

  • #16348 7d26cd7 Thanks @​ocavue! - Fixes a bug where emitted assets during a client build would contain always fresh, new hashes in their name. Now the build should be more stable.

  • #16317 d012bfe Thanks @​das-peter! - Fixes a bug where allowedDomains weren't correctly propagated when using the development server.

  • #16379 5a84551 Thanks @​martrapp! - Improves Vue scoped style handling in DEV mode during client router navigation.

  • #16317 d012bfe Thanks @​das-peter! - Adds tests to verify settings are properly propagated when using the development server.

  • #16282 5b0fdaa Thanks @​jmurty! - Fixes build errors on platforms with skew protection enabled (e.g. Vercel, Netlify) for inter-chunk Javascript using dynamic imports

  • Updated dependencies [e0b240e]:

    • @​astrojs/telemetry@​3.3.1

astro@6.1.7

Patch Changes

  • #16027 c62516b Thanks @​fkatsuhiro! - Fixes a bug where remote image dimensions were not validated during static builds on Netlify.

  • #16311 94048f2 Thanks @​Arecsu! - Fixes --port flag being ignored after a Vite-triggered server restart (e.g. when a .env file changes)

  • #16316 0fcd04c Thanks @​ematipico! - Fixes the /_image endpoint accepting an arbitrary f=svg query parameter and serving non-SVG content as image/svg+xml. The endpoint now validates that the source is actually SVG before honoring f=svg, matching the same guard already enforced on the <Image> component path.

  • #14924 bb4586a Thanks @​aralroca! - Fixes SCSS and CSS module file changes triggering a full page reload instead of hot-updating styles in place during development

title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [HIGH] Fix XSS vulnerability in Card component
body:
🚨 Severity: HIGH
💡 Vulnerability: The `<Card />` Astro component accepted an `href` prop and outputted it directly into an anchor tag without sanitization. This allowed the potential for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if malicious URLs (like `javascript:alert(1)`) were passed to the component.
🎯 Impact: Malicious actors could potentially inject execution of arbitrary JavaScript if they can control the `href` input.
🔧 Fix: Imported the existing `sanitizeUrl` function from `src/lib/security.js` and applied it to the `href` attribute in `src/components/Card.astro`.
✅ Verification: Ran `bun test` and verified all tests pass, including the `security.test.js` suite.


---
*PR created automatically by Jules for task [2590579485755381639](https://jules.google.com/task/2590579485755381639) started by @jgeofil*

title: ⚡ Bolt: Extract regexes to module-level constants in security utility
body:
💡 **What:** Extracted inline regular expressions `[\x00-\x1F\x7F-\x9F]`, `\s+`, and `:` from the `sanitizeUrl` function to module-level constants `CONTROL_CHARS_REGEX`, `WHITESPACE_REGEX`, and `COLON_ENTITY_REGEX` in `src/lib/security.js`.

🎯 **Why:** To optimize performance by avoiding redundant Regular Expression object compilation and allocation on every call to `sanitizeUrl`. Hoisting them to the module scope ensures they are compiled exactly once.

📊 **Impact:** Minor reduction in memory churn and CPU overhead for string processing, leading to more efficient execution without sacrificing readability.

🔬 **Measurement:** The `bun test src/lib/security.test.js` test suite continues to pass successfully, verifying that no regressions were introduced to the URL sanitization logic.

---

title: 🔒 Prevent sensitive data exposure in Vercel analytics
body:
### 🎯 What
The vulnerability fixed is sensitive data exposure via full URL tracking in Vercel analytics.

### ⚠️ Risk
If left unfixed, sensitive information such as API tokens, session IDs, or PII contained in URL query parameters or hashes could be leaked to the analytics provider and potentially exposed in logs or dashboards.

### 🛡️ Solution
The fix involves sanitizing the `href` field in the analytics payload by using `location.origin + location.pathname` instead of `location.href`. This ensures only the base URL and path are tracked, stripping away any sensitive parameters or fragments.

title: ⚡ Bolt: Optimize path parameter replacement in vitals.js
body:
### ⚡ Bolt: Optimize path parameter replacement in vitals.js

#### 💡 What
I have optimized the `sendToAnalytics` function in `src/lib/vitals.js` by replacing the `Object.entries(options.params).reduce(...)` pattern with a manual `for` loop iterating over `Object.keys(params)`.

#### 🎯 Why
The original implementation was suboptimal for a performance-critical tracking path. `Object.entries()` creates a new array of arrays, and `reduce()` incurs the overhead of a callback function for every parameter. In a browser environment, especially during page load (when web-vitals are reported), minimizing these allocations and function calls is beneficial.

#### 📊 Impact
Measured a **~1.8x to 2.0x performance improvement** in execution time for the path replacement logic. It also reduces short-lived object allocations, which helps maintain a smoother main thread during metric reporting.

#### 🧪 Measurement
I established a baseline using a benchmark script (`src/lib/vitals.benchmark.js`) that simulated various parameter counts (Empty, Small, Medium, Large). 

**Benchmark Results (1,000,000 iterations):**
- **Original (`reduce`):** 1262.01ms
- **Optimized (`for` loop):** 697.32ms
- **Improvement:** ~45% reduction in execution time.

#### ✅ Verification
- **Correctness:** Verified using a dedicated test suite (`src/lib/vitals.test.js`) covering multiple edge cases (empty params, null params, multiple replacements) to ensure identical behavior to the original implementation.
- **Code Health:** Updated `.jules/bolt.md` with performance-specific learnings.

title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [CRITICAL] Fix XSS Bypass in URL Sanitizer via Control Characters
body:
🚨 Severity: CRITICAL
💡 Vulnerability: The `sanitizeUrl` utility relied on the `URL` constructor to identify and block dangerous protocols like `javascript:`. However, the `URL` parser throws an error if the URL contains unescaped whitespace or control characters in its scheme (e.g., `java script:alert(1)` or `j\x00avascript:alert(1)`). When the parser threw, the fallback logic checked for relative paths via `!url.includes('://')` and incorrectly allowed these malicious payloads as "safe" relative URLs. Browsers ignore these control characters and will still execute the XSS payload.
🎯 Impact: Attackers could bypass XSS protections using control characters and execute arbitrary JavaScript by injecting malicious `javascript:` URIs into user-controlled dynamic links.
🔧 Fix: Updated `sanitizeUrl` to proactively strip all control characters and spaces (`[\x00-\x20]`), lowercase the string, and explicitly check for `javascript:`, `data:`, and `vbscript:` protocols using `.startsWith()` *before* invoking the `URL` constructor or relying on the fallback logic.
✅ Verification: Added a comprehensive unit test suite in `src/lib/security.test.js` to ensure bypasses using spaces, null bytes, tabs, newlines, and mixed casing are correctly blocked, and valid URLs remain unimpacted. Run `bun test` to verify.

title: Fix Astro build failure in middleware resolution
body:
The build failed on Vercel because Vite/Rollup could not resolve the virtual module `astro:middleware`. This is a known issue when migrating Astro projects to versions 3.x and above if the manual step of updating `src/env.d.ts` is skipped. The previous reference to `astro/client-image` was deprecated and replaced by the unified `astro/client` types. title: ⚡ Bolt: Optimize profile image using Astro Image component
body:
💡 What:
Upgraded the profile image (`public/profile.jpg`) to use Astro's native `<Image />` component by moving it to `src/assets/profile.jpg`. Applied `loading="eager"` since it's an above-the-fold LCP element.

🎯 Why:
Images in the `public` directory bypass Astro's build pipeline and are served as-is. By importing the image as a local asset and using `<Image />`, Astro automatically optimizes it (resizes, converts to modern formats like WebP/AVIF) during the build. This reduces the final bundle size. Using `loading="eager"` prevents the browser from delaying the load of the Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) element. Explicit width and height are maintained to prevent Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).

📊 Impact:
- Reduces the image payload size served to the client via WebP/AVIF format conversion and resizing.
- Improves Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) time.
- Prevents CLS.

🔬 Measurement:
- Run `bun run build` and inspect the output directory (`dist/`) or `.vercel/output/static/_image`. You will see the optimized hashed WebP/AVIF image generated by Astro instead of the raw `profile.jpg`.
- Check Lighthouse performance scores or Chrome DevTools Network tab to observe the reduced image transfer size.

title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [HIGH] Fix XSS bypass in URL sanitization
body:
🚨 **Severity:** HIGH
💡 **Vulnerability:** The `sanitizeUrl` function in `src/lib/security.js` relied solely on `.trim()` and `.startsWith()` checks. This allowed attackers to bypass the XSS filter using non-standard control characters (e.g., `\x01javascript:alert(1)`) or internal spacing (e.g., `java\nscript:`) which browsers still parse as valid protocols.
🎯 **Impact:** If an attacker provided a crafted URL with control characters, it would bypass the sanitization check and execute arbitrary JavaScript when clicked by a user, leading to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).
🔧 **Fix:** Updated the `sanitizeUrl` function to use the built-in `URL` constructor (which mirrors browser parsing behavior and handles control characters) along with a robust fallback regex to explicitly strip `^[\s\x00-\x1F]+` before checking the protocol.
✅ **Verification:** Ran test scripts validating that variations like `\x01javascript:` and `java\nscript:` are correctly sanitized to `#`.

Logged a new entry in `.jules/sentinel.md` noting the vulnerability and prevention strategy.

---

title: ⚡ Bolt: Prevent Cumulative Layout Shift for component images
body: ### 💡 What: Added explicit `width` and `height` attributes to the `` tags in `LinkGrid.astro` and `SocialGrid.astro` components based on their defined CSS sizes. ### 🎯 Why: Images without explicit dimensions cause Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) because the browser cannot reserve the correct space for them before they finish loading. This leads to content jumping around during the initial page load, which negatively impacts the CLS Core Web Vital metric. ### 📊 Impact: Improves the CLS score. By providing the explicit dimensions to the browser, it can allocate the exact space needed for the images, completely eliminating any layout shift caused by these assets as they stream in. ### 🔬 Measurement: Run performance audits (e.g., Lighthouse, PageSpeed Insights) on pages utilizing these components (like the homepage and CV page). The CLS metric should show an improvement (closer to 0) as these images will no longer contribute to layout shifts. --- *PR created automatically by Jules for task [3317409009721248258](https://jules.google.com/task/3317409009721248258) started by @jgeofil* ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Bug Fixes** * Image components now use explicit width and height attributes, improving layout stability and preventing visual shifting. * **Documentation** * Added guidance on specifying explicit image dimensions in components to maintain visual consistency.

🎨 Palette: Add focus visible styles and delightful hover states
body:
💡 What: 
- Added `aria-hidden="true"` to purely decorative arrow spans (`→`) in `Card.astro`.
- Implemented a clear, consistent, and global `:focus-visible` outline for `<a>` tags in `Layout.astro`.
- Added a delightful hover/focus-visible scale transition (`transform: scale(1.1)`) for icons in `SocialGrid.astro`.

🎯 Why: 
- Screen readers previously announced the decorative arrow symbol ("rightwards arrow"), causing unnecessary noise for users relying on assistive technologies.
- The default browser focus states can often be too subtle or inconsistent, making keyboard navigation difficult for users who rely on it. A dedicated `:focus-visible` state ensures an explicit and highly visible outline when navigating via keyboard.
- Micro-interactions, like a subtle scale on hover, make the interface feel more responsive, polished, and pleasant to use, confirming user intent before a click.

📸 Before/After: 
*(Tested locally via Playwright headless testing on hover and focus events)*
- Social icons now scale up elegantly when hovered or focused.
- All links now receive a distinct 2px solid border on keyboard focus.

♿ Accessibility: 
- **Screen Reader Improvement:** Purely decorative text elements are now hidden from the accessibility tree, cleaning up the auditory experience.
- **Keyboard Navigation:** Explicit visual focus indicators support WCAG Success Criterion 2.4.7 (Focus Visible), ensuring users can always tell which element currently holds keyboard focus.

title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [HIGH] Fix sensitive data exposure in analytics
body:
🚨 Severity: HIGH
💡 Vulnerability: The web vitals reporting logic in `src/lib/vitals.js` was reading the raw `location.href` value and directly appending it to the analytics payload sent to Vercel Analytics. This raw `href` could contain sensitive information like query parameters or URL fragment hashes.
🎯 Impact: Unintentional leaking of sensitive data (PII, reset tokens, auth tokens) to external analytics platforms, compromising user privacy and account security.
🔧 Fix: Sanitized the analytics endpoint URL tracking to use `location.origin + location.pathname`, effectively stripping out any potentially sensitive query parameters or URL hashes before transmission.
✅ Verification: Ran `bun run build` and `bun test` to ensure there are no regressions. Verified locally that the payload does not contain `?token=...` styled strings.

title: 🔒 [security fix] Fix potential XSS via unvalidated URLs
body:
🎯 **What:** This PR fixes a potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability by sanitizing URLs used in `href` attributes across several components.

⚠️ **Risk:** Without sanitization, malicious protocols like `javascript:`, `data:`, or `vbscript:` could be injected into the application's data sources (e.g., `cv.json`). When a user clicks a compromised link, arbitrary JavaScript could be executed in their browser, potentially leading to session theft or other malicious actions.

🛡️ **Solution:**
1.  **New Utility:** Added `src/lib/security.js` which exports a `sanitizeUrl` function. This function checks for dangerous protocols and returns `about:blank` if any are detected.
2.  **Component Updates:** Updated `Funding.astro`, `Project.astro`, `LinkGrid.astro`, and `SocialGrid.astro` to wrap their `href` values with `sanitizeUrl`.
3.  **Verification:** Added unit tests in `src/lib/security.test.js` covering valid URLs, relative paths, and various dangerous protocols. All tests pass using `bun test`.
4.  **Cleanup:** Removed the auto-generated `.astro/` directory to ensure a clean build and avoid committing artifacts.

✅ **Verification:**
- Ran `bun test`: All 6 tests for `sanitizeUrl` passed.
- Code review confirmed the approach as a robust fix for the identified vulnerability.

🎯 **Why:** Astro does not correctly split chunks for statically imported scripts evaluated conditionally via environment variables. 📊 **Impact:** Reduces the initial main bundle size significantly. Previously, `Layout.astro` chunks included all Statsig dependencies. With dynamic imports, the main chunk size decreases and the dependencies are lazily loaded.

🔬 **Measurement:** Verified via `bun run build`. The build output shows the generation of an empty chunk or significantly smaller chunks for `Layout.astro` initial script, deferring the load to dynamically loaded chunks. Started dev server and verified site renders properly.

🚨 **Severity**: HIGH
💡 **Vulnerability**: The application was using `location.href` to pass the page URL to external analytics services. This included query strings and hashes, which could inadvertently leak sensitive data (like PII, authentication tokens, or search terms) to those third-party services.
🎯 **Impact**: Sending full URLs to external services can expose sensitive data in a way that violates privacy policies and data security standards.
🔧 **Fix**: Updated `src/lib/vitals.js` to use `location.origin + location.pathname` instead of `location.href`. This ensures that only the base URL and path are sent to the analytics service, stripping out any potentially sensitive query parameters or hashes. Added an inline code comment explaining the security concern.
✅ **Verification**: Verified the change by running the build (`bun run build`) and ensuring it completes successfully without errors. Evaluated tests (`bun test`) and found no regressions.

title: ⚡ Bolt: Optimize Statsig imports for code splitting
body:
💡 What: Refactored the Statsig analytics, session replay, and JS client static imports to use dynamic imports `Promise.all([import(...)])` within the conditionally evaluated `statsigKey` block in `src/layouts/Layout.astro`.

🎯 Why: Astro does not properly code-split conditionally evaluated static imports within scripts, leading to the entire Statsig bundle loading synchronously in the main thread layout. By dynamically importing these non-critical third-party analytics scripts, we prevent render-blocking and significantly reduce the initial JavaScript payload size.

📊 Impact: Reduces the initial layout JS payload by avoiding synchronous loading of `@statsig/js-client`, `@statsig/session-replay`, and `@statsig/web-analytics`.

🔬 Measurement: Verifiable by inspecting the generated Vite build chunks (via `bun run build`) or Chrome DevTools network tab. The Statsig chunks will now correctly split into asynchronous lazy-loaded files rather than being bundled in the primary layout chunk.
